@@ -202,7 +202,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ad","title":"Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)","description":"# Implement FUSE mount lifecycle (mount/unmount/signal handling)

## GOAL
Implement robust mount lifecycle management with clean startup, shutdown, and signal handling.

## BACKGROUND
Production filesystems must handle:
- Clean mount with validation
- Graceful unmount with flush
- Signal handling (SIGTERM, SIGINT)
- Crash recovery on restart

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Mount Phase
```rust
pub async fn mount(config: MountConfig, cx: &Cx) -> Result<MountHandle> {
    // 1. Validate image (superblock, journal state)
    // 2. Replay journal if needed (ext4)
    // 3. Initialize MVCC store
    // 4. Initialize ARC cache
    // 5. Initialize repair manager
    // 6. Start background tasks (scrub, GC)
    // 7. Mount FUSE session
    // 8. Return handle for lifecycle control
}
```

### 2. Unmount Phase
```rust
impl MountHandle {
    pub async fn unmount(self, cx: &Cx) -> Result<()> {
        // 1. Stop accepting new requests
        // 2. Wait for in-flight requests (with timeout)
        // 3. Flush dirty cache entries
        // 4. Commit pending transactions
        // 5. Refresh repair symbols for dirty groups
        // 6. Close FUSE session
        // 7. Sync and close device
    }
}
```

### 3. Signal Handling
- SIGTERM/SIGINT: Trigger graceful unmount
- SIGUSR1: Dump debug state to log
- Timeout for unmount (default 30s, then force)

### 4. Drop Safety
- MountHandle::drop must trigger unmount
- Panic in unmount must not corrupt filesystem
- Leaked handle must eventually timeout

## TESTS
1. Unit: Mount/unmount roundtrip
2. Integration: SIGTERM during I/O results in clean state
3. Integration: Double-mount same image fails cleanly
4. Stress: Mount/unmount loop 100x without leaks

## LOGGING
- Mount: log config, image path, mount point
- Unmount: log flush stats, pending ops
- Signal: log signal received, action taken

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. `ffs mount ... && ffs umount ...` leaves clean state
2. `kill -TERM $(pidof ffs)` results in clean unmount
3. Remount after crash shows no corruption
4. No resource leaks (file descriptors, memory)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:50:34.171965858Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310382109Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T10:14:20.310314994Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["fuse","lifecycle","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-20p","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:06.032850244Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ad","depends_on_id":"bd-2s4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:37.263209229Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":26,"issue_id":"bd-2ad","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"FEATURE_PARITY mapping: This bead covers the 'FUSE mount runtime' row (currently ❌). When complete, flip to ✅ and update ParityReport::current() counts.","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:53:49Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2c1y","title":"MVCC durability: persist WAL before exposing commit","description":"Context: current PersistentMvccStore commit flow applies to in-memory store before WAL append, creating crash window where acknowledged commit is lost on replay.\n\nScope:\n- Reorder commit pipeline so WAL append/sync succeeds before in-memory visibility of commit.\n- Ensure WAL append failure returns error and does not expose transaction versions.\n- Add regression tests for WAL failure path and replay durability.\n\nAcceptance:\n- commit() does not mutate visible store state when WAL append fails.\n- successful commit remains replayable after restart.\n- targeted tests pass via rch.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-19T02:07:05.755076103Z","created_by":"SilverLake","updated_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:03.539117230Z","closed_at":"2026-02-19T02:21:03.539092574Z","close_reason":"Completed: WAL failure now rolls back in-memory commit state; mvcc durability tests and workspace gates pass via rch","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["correctness","durability","mvcc","wal"]}

-{"id":"bd-devbv","title":"add mounted-path E2E and ioctl-trace coverage for ext4 EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION","description":"Survey finding: `FsOps::get_inode_version` exists in `crates/ffs-core/src/vfs.rs`, `ffs-fuse` dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION`, and `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` has direct dispatch tests, but `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` has no mounted-path proof for the ioctl.

Scope:
- Add a mounted-path helper in `fuse_e2e.rs` that issues `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION` against a real ext4 file over FUSE.
- Assert the ioctl-trace log records the command when the kernel forwards it, and soft-skip only when the kernel/VFS rejects before userspace dispatch.
- Verify stable value encoding and buffer-size/errno behavior against the mounted path, not only the unit-dispatch shim.
- Update `FEATURE_PARITY.md` if operator-visible coverage now exists.

Acceptance:
- At least one mounted-path `EXT4_IOC_GETVERSION` scenario exists with deterministic assertions.
- CI artifacts make it clear whether failures are transport-layer skips or FrankenFS regressions.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:09.486713081Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T15:01:12.103524455Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuse","ioctl","testing"]}

@@ -154,6 +154,7 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::set_inode_flags` with user-settable flag masking (`EXT4_USER_SETTABLE_FLAGS`). System flags (EXTENTS, HUGE_FILE, etc.) protected. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS` (0x40086602), requires write mode. Direct `ffs-fuse` dispatch coverage verifies write-scope routing and masked flag persistence; mounted-path E2E in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now covers generic flag roundtrips plus ext4 `EXT4_COMPR_FL` enable-on-empty-file behavior on COMPRESSION-feature images, post-ioctl compressed write/readback, and stable `EOPNOTSUPP` rejection when the COMPRESSION incompat bit is absent. The tests still soft-skip when the current kernel/FUSE stack rejects the write ioctl with `ENOTTY` before userspace dispatch. |
+| FUSE ioctl FS_IOC_GETFSLABEL / FS_IOC_SETFSLABEL | `fs/ioctl.c`, ext4 superblock volume name field | ✅ | `OpenFs::get_fs_label` now rereads the ext4 superblock on each request so mounted and direct-image callers observe post-mutation labels instead of the open-time cache. `OpenFs::set_fs_label` rewrites `s_volume_name`, preserves the ext4 16-byte limit, rejects interior NULs with `EINVAL`, and recomputes the superblock checksum when `metadata_csum` is enabled. `ffs-fuse` dispatches `FS_IOC_GETFSLABEL` (0x81009431) and `FS_IOC_SETFSLABEL` (0x41009432), enforces the 256-byte userspace buffer contract, rejects unterminated setter payloads with `EINVAL`, and commits through the normal write request scope. `crates/ffs-core` unit tests cover direct ext4 mutation and reopen persistence; `crates/ffs-fuse` unit tests cover getter sizing plus setter routing; `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now proves mounted-path ext4 label updates survive remount, with direct-image verification fallback when the current kernel/FUSE stack blocks remount `GETFSLABEL` before userspace dispatch. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_MOVE_EXT | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c`, kernel ext4 admin-guide ioctl table | ✅ | `ffs-fuse` parses and validates `struct move_extent`, resolves the caller's donor fd from `/proc/<pid>/fd/<n>`, verifies the donor stays on the mounted filesystem, temporarily registers the donor inode for `OpenFs::move_ext`, and emits structured `operation_id` / `scenario_id` / `outcome` / `error_class` logs for success and rejection. `crates/ffs-core` keeps mkfs-backed backend tests for `EBADF`, short moved-length-at-EOF behavior, hole rejection, and middle-range extent swapping, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now proves mounted-path middle-range exchange plus hole-backed rejection over a real ext4 FUSE mount with ioctl-trace evidence. |
 | CLI inspect command | FrankenFS spec §6 | ✅ | Implemented in `ffs-cli` |
 | CLI info command | FrankenFS spec §14.2 | ✅ | `ffs info` implemented in `ffs-cli` with optional `--groups`, `--mvcc`, `--repair`, and `--journal` sections plus `--json` output |
